sections.paragraph:
ἐνσκιρόω pf. pass. ptc. ἐνεσκιρωμένος (σκιρόομαι ‘become hard’; X., De Re Equ. 4, 2; Stoic. III p. 102, 38; Is 27:1 Theod.; Etym. Mag. p. 344, 30) prim. mng. ‘to cause to become hard’; in our lit. of moral insensitivity, harden, make callous Hv 3, 9, 8.—DELG s.v. σκῖρος.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

prim. prim. = primarily

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 339
